free_text: A study of the 14N(d,p)15N reaction at low bombarding
energies.
Energy spread of the beam was less than 1.5 keV.
The proton spectra were measured with an ORTEC
SBCJ-25-500 semiconductor detector. Another
semiconductor detector mounted on a target chamber at
an angle of 90 degrees was used as a monitor.
Faraday cup was connected to a current integrator.
Proton magnetic resonance system was used for measuring
of the deuteron beam.
Gas cell filled with air of 20 mm Hg pressure was used.
Foils of Al2O3 of about 65 mu-g/cm2 thickness served as
entrance and exit windows. At Ed=0.6 MeV the thickness
of the gas target was about 5 keV.
Uncertainty in the effective bombarding energy,
primarily due to the changes in the thickness of the
entrance window if the targets, was 3 keV.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
Error from the graphic bar. The statistical
errors are generally smaller than the size of dots in
the figure except some cases.
Determination of the absolute cross section had an
additional error of 5% to the statical one.
The error of absolute cross sections is estimated to be
about 8%.

Fig.2
Solid adenine (C5H5N5) target of sandwich type was
used. A thin adenine layer was evaporated onto a nickel
backing (about 100 mu-g/cm2), then it was covered by a
copper layer (about 100 mu-g/cm2) to prevent the
adenine from escaping during the bombardment. The
thickness of the adenine was about 30 mu-g/cm2 (about
25 keV) at Ed=0.6 MeV and was increased to 50 mu-g/cm2
at the lowest energy.
Results of fitting the experimental points by
the Legendre polynomials using the method of least
squares.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
Errors from graphic bar represent the error
of the angular distribution measurement which is less
than 5 %.
